---
title: "Week1"
author: "Maartje"
date: 26-04-2026
format: pdf
---
# Problem 1 
```{r}
library(PeacoQC)
```
# Answer 1

#PeacoQC will determine peaks on the channels in the flowframe. Then it will remove anomalies caused by e.g. clogs, changes in speed etc. by using an IsolationTree and/or the MAD method. 

# Problem 2 
Take a closer look at the list of Bioconductor cytometry packages. Report back on how many there are currently in Bioconductor, the author/maintainer with the most contributed cytometry R packages, and a couple packages that you would be interested in exploring more in-depth later in the course.

# answer 2
69
Mike Jiang
flowAI
flowMerge
flowCyBar

# problem 3
![](c:\Users\maart\MaartjeCyto\Week 1\Images\image.png)
![](c:\Users\maart\MaartjeCyto\Week 1\Images\Install broom.png)
For installing with pak you don't need to know where the package is stored (CRAN/Bioconductor or github). Also the summary of how the installation process was done in the console is much more concise compared to install() install.packages() or install_github
![](c:\Users\maart\MaartjeCyto\Week 1\Images\Installing cytomem.png)
I have an error for installing CytoExploreR and I don't know how to fix that
![](c:\Users\maart\MaartjeCyto\Week 1\Images\Error CytoExploreR.png)
